

## 1. Summary
**Entra Auth Log Analyzer** is a lightweight security analysis application designed to work completely offline in an air gaped environment to process and analyse authentication telemetry generated by **Microsoft Entra ID**.

The application enables security analysts and administrators to transform raw authentication logs into structured insights that support identity threat detection, incident investigation, and operational monitoring.

The system follows a **client-side analytical architecture**, the logs are analyzed completely in memory/browser session, no logs are stored on the server, prioritizing privacy, simplicity,  and rapid analysis without dependency on external SIEM infrastructure.

## 2. High-Level Architecture
```code 
┌───────────────────────────┐  
│ Admin Imports MS Entra ID │  
│ Authentication Logs       │  
└──────────────┬────────────┘  
               │  
               ▼  
┌───────────────────────────┐  
│ Data Ingestion            │  
│ (Log Upload / Import)     │  
└──────────────┬────────────┘  
               │  
               ▼  
┌───────────────────────────┐  
│ Parsing & Normalization   │  
└──────────────┬────────────┘  
               │  
               ▼  
┌───────────────────────────┐  
│ Enrichment & Analytics    │  
└──────────────┬────────────┘  
               │  
               ▼  
┌───────────────────────────┐  
│ Visualization & Reporting │  
└───────────────────────────┘
```
## 3. Architectural Components
### 3.1 Log Source Layer
The authentication data originates from **Microsoft Entra ID sign-in audit logs**, which contain identity events such as: user authentication attempts, success/Failed logins and location user logged-in

The user will need to have this logs handy before they can be uploaded into the application. The application does not connect to the Microsoft Entra itself, it will just parse the .csv file for an offline analysis. The analyse is done locally no internet is required to run the application. 
### 3.2 Data Ingestion Layer
The ingestion layer is responsible for importing authentication datasets into the application.
- Accepts .csv files
- Entra Interactive Sign logs
- No backend dependency
- Offline analysis capability
- Minimal setup requirements
### 3.3 Parsing & Normalization Layer
Raw Entra logs contain nested structures
This layer helps normalize the data extracts relevant authentication fields and normalizes timestamps and identities

### 3.4 Enrichment & Analytics Engine
Entra Log Analyzer correlated identity activity with geographical and behavioral context to highlight suspicious authentication patterns such: success/failure aggregation, user-to-IP correlation, geographic and ASN enrichment, application access targeting analysis, behavioral anomaly detection, risk scoring heuristics.

### 3.5 Visualization Layer
The visualization layer enables rapid triage by transforming large authentication datasets into actionable insights
Capabilities include:
- Authentication timelines
- Auth flow chart
- Impossible travel detection
- Password spray
- Brute force patterns
- Rapid authentication sequences
- Attempting to access restricted apps
- Abnormal login frequency
- User activity summaries
- Failure distribution charts
- Risk severity visualization
- Investigation filtering views

## 4. Data Flow
- Administrator exports authentication logs from Entra ID.
- Logs are uploaded into EntraAuthLogAnalyzer.
- Parser extracts and standardizes authentication events.
- Analytics engine enriches and evaluates activity. 
- Results are rendered in dashboards for investigation.
- Findings may be exported for further analysis tools.

## 5. Deployment Model

The application follows a **local-first deployment model**:

|Characteristic|Approach|
|---|---|
|Hosting|Static / Local execution|
|Backend|Not required|
|Data Processing|Client-side|
|Data Storage|In-memory / browser session|
|External Dependencies|Optional enrichment sources|

### 6. Network
The application does not need network connectivity to work. All the analysis is done offline and locally. The data is processed on memory and not saved on the server. 
## 7. Security
This design aligns with identity-forensics use cases where log confidentiality is critical.
The architecture minimizes exposure risks by:
- Performing analysis locally
- Avoiding persistent storage of authentication logs
- Eliminating external API transmission by default
- Supporting offline forensic workflows
### 8. Access
We can control who can access the application by restricting by the Prisma IP, and then controlling the users who can access the app using the Prisma Build in ACL.
Because the application only process data, whoever uses it will need to provide its own data set for the analysis. Therefore, its possible to host this application within our internal network for any of our colleagues to use.

### 9. Architectural Principles
The system design follows these principles:
- Privacy-first analytics
- Infrastructure independence
- Minimal operational overhead
